vulnerability.reason: generated describes a lack of verification processes, whereas the reference specifies low eHealth literacy as the sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Low eHealth literacy prevents users from critically evaluating AI-generated health reports.
vulnerability.generated: Users lack verification processes to detect semantic errors in LLM-generated reports from structured data.

impact.reason: affected party (users) is present, but damage type is generic erroneous decisions instead of the specific health decisions based on factually incorrect medical reports.
impact.reference: Users make health decisions based on factually incorrect AI-generated medical reports.
impact.generated: Erroneous decisions and misinformation spread from users acting on semantically inaccurate generated text.

dose_response.reason: causal arc starts with semantic accuracy degradation and ends with erroneous decisions, but the generated introduces a lack of verification processes as an intermediate step not present in the reference, altering the causal logic.
dose_response.reference: Semantic accuracy degradation enables high-rate semantic errors, which in open-domain data-to-text generation exposes users relying on LLM-generated reports, leading to health decisions based on factually incorrect AI-generated medical reports.
dose_response.generated: Semantic accuracy degradation in LLM text generation from structured data, through users relying on these reports, triggers a lack of verification processes to detect semantic errors, leading to erroneous decisions and misinformation spreading.
